channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Obanikoro rules out Lagos governorship bid, insists he won 2007 poll
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Obanikoro said his governorship ambition was now “completely off the table” during an appearance on Mic On Podcast, hosted by Channels Television journalist Seun Okinbaloye.

item.title: ‎“You Have No Business Relocating Abroad If You’re In Your 50s” – Singer Speed Darlington
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: ‎Nigerian British rapper Speed Darlington has addressed those in their 50s who want to relocate abroad. ‎In a video captured online, he told them they have no business relocating abroad. He questioned their purpose in going there, wondering if they wanted to hustle at such an age. ‎Speed said that b

item.title: World Cup Final Deadlocked As Spain, Argentina Cancel Each Other Out At Half-Time
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Spain and Argentina remain locked at 0-0 at half-time in the 2026 FIFA World Cup final after a tense and closely contested opening 45 minutes at the New York New Jersey Stadium. Neither side was able to break the deadlock despite enjoying spells of possession in a cagey first half, with both defence

item.title: 2027: Alake endorses Adebutu for Ogun governorship
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: According to him, while members were making financial contributions towards acquiring the property, Chief Adebutu asked them to keep their money and personally paid for the building. The post 2027: Alake endorses Adebutu for Ogun governorship appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: ‎Stella Damasus Leaves Many Confused Over Striking Resemblance With Second Daughter As Toolz, Others, React
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: ‎Nollywood actress Stella Damasus has left many gushing over a video of herself and her second daughter, Angelica Aboderin. ‎The actress shared a video on Instagram of her and her mini-me engaging in a Q&A. ‎In the clip, the mother-daughter duo sat side by side as they answered questions about w

item.title: BREAKING: Senegal’s Faye Emerges New ECOWAS Chairman
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Senegalese President Bassirou Diomaye Faye has emerged as the new Chairman of the Authority of Heads of State and Government of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS), succeeding Sierra Leone’s President Julius Maada Bio. The leadership change was announced at the ECOWAS Author

item.title: [BREAKING] Senegal President Elected ECOWAS Chairman
item.dc:creator: Channels TV
item.description: Faye emerged as the new chairman during the 69th Ordinary Session of the Authority of Heads of State and Government of the Economic Community of West African States (ECOWAS) in Sierra Leone. The post [BREAKING] Senegal President Elected ECOWAS Chairman appeared first on Channels Television.

item.title: NBA Election Sparks New Controversy As Akinboro, Akangbe Reject Badejo-Okusanya’s Victory
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The 2026 Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) national election has ignited a fresh controversy, as two of the three presidential candidates, Aare Olumuyiwa Akinboro (SAN) and Lateef Akangbe (SAN), challenged the legitimacy of Oyinkansola Badejo-Okusanya (SAN) as the winner of the highly contested poll. B

item.title: 'My hands were all over the place!' - Fox savours dramatic Open win
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Ryan Fox admitted it was a "dream come true" as he defied the nerves to clinch a dramatic victory in The Open at Royal Birkdale.

item.title: DeChambeau 'not a cheater' - Scheffler defends Bryson over rules drama
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Scottie Scheffler has defended Bryson DeChambeau over his rules controversy at The Open and has insisted the two-time US Open champion is ‘not a cheater’.

item.description: The celebrity line-up will continue at half-time with performances by Madonna, Justin Bieber, Shakira and BTS.

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Klopp confirms next managerial job
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Liverpool manager, Jurgen Klopp, has confirmed he would be confirmed as the new head coach of Germany “in the next few days”. Klopp is set to replace Julian Nagelsmann who was in charge for the 2026 World Cup. Nagelsmann has already left the role following a poor outing at the tou

item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Rate the Spain and Argentina players out of 10 below and come back 30 minutes after full-time to see the final ratings.

item.title: Claims Tinubu, Oyetola Abandon Osun APC Guber Candidate False – Oyintiloye
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: A chieftain of the All Progressives Congress (APC) in Osun State, Hon. Olatunbosun Oyintiloye, has dismissed a report alleging that President Bola Tinubu and the Minister of Marine and Blue Economy, Mr Gboyega Oyetola, have abandoned the party’s candidate in the August 15 governorship election, Mr B

item.title: NPFL to organise marketing workshops for clubs ahead new season
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Nigeria Premier Football League (NPFL) has announced a mandatory Commercial, Marketing and Media Workshop for all clubs ahead of the 2026/2027 season. The league body made the announcement in a statement addressed to all clubs. According to the statement, each club must be represented by two off

item.title: 2027: NDC will win before 12 noon in free, fair election – Fayose
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Isaac Fayose, younger brother to former Governor Ayodele Fayose of Ekiti state says the Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, will win the 2027 presidential polls in a free and fair election. Fayose made this remark in a post on his Facebook page on Sunday. He was reacting to Lere Olayinka, media aide t

item.description: Argentina captain, Lionel Messi, is set to equal a record held by Brazilian legend Ronaldo Nazario if he wins the 2026 World Cup final with his country on Sunday night. item.title: Dozens missing after passenger ferry sinks off coast of Guyana
item.dc:creator: The Guardian
item.description: Emergency response has rescued 67 people, including 15 children, from one of the country’s worst maritime disastersA ferry carrying 116 passengers and crew sank ⁠off the coast of Guyana in South America ⁠late Saturday, with 67 ⁠people, ​including 15 children, rescued so far, according to Guyana ⁠aut

item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of the 247 Reconnaissance Battalion have intercepted 15 jerrycans of Premium Motor Spirit, PMS, popularly known as petrol, during a fighting patrol in Mubi North Local Government Area of Adamawa State. item.description: Following the success of its $99 Kodak-branded Snapic A1, Reto Project is releasing the Kodak EC35, an even more affordable 35mm film camera for just $34.99. The EC35 certainly isn't fancy. Its 25mm acrylic lens with a fixed f/10 aperture and 1/100 shutter speed basically put it on par with a drugst

item.title: Duckett, Curran the difference as England clinch India series in Lord's run-fest
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Ben Duckett's century and Sam Curran's late burst of wickets inspired England to a series-clinching 27-run win over India in the third ODI at Lord's.

item.title: Northern Group Warns Against Attempts To Use NNPCL To Divide Nigerians
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Concerned Northern Forum has urged Nigerians to reject narratives capable of pitching the North against the South,warning some people to stop using the affairs of the Nigerian National Petroleum Company Limited (NNPC Ltd) to sow ethnic and regional divisions. Addressing a news conference in Kad

item.title: ‘Sacred unity’: Iran’s supreme leader calls for calm amid political divides
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Decree comes after hardline backers of the Iranian government accused officials of treason for signing an MoU with US.

item.title: Super Eagles: 40-cap Nigerian defender handed Turkish escape route after Nantes relegation
item.dc:creator: Soccernet NG
item.description: Super Eagles defender Chidozie Awaziem could be set for a swift return to top-flight football after Turkish Super Lig side Konyaspor opened talks over a summer move, Soccernet.ng reports. The 29-year-old centre-back endured a difficult campaign with French club Nantes last season, as the Canaries su

item.title: At least 50 Malian soldiers killed in attack on military convoy
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: At least 50 Malian soldiers were killed after a joint Malian army and Russia's Africa Corps convoy was ambushed.

item.title: Corps members, police rig elections, not INEC chair – Deji Adeleke
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “You cannot blame that on the INEC chairman when youth corps members are collecting ₦50,000 and changing figures at the polling unit,” Adeleke said.

item.title: 7 Suspected Kidnappers Arrested In Anambra
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Seven suspected kidnappers involved in separate kidnapping and armed robbery incidents across Anambra state have been arrested by the Police Command. In the afternoon of 15th July 2026, Police Operatives attached to the Rapid Response Squad (RRS), Awkuzu, arrested one Okoye Ibegbunam Chukwuebuka Joh

item.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Eight people die and dozens are injured as several cities are hit by missile and drone attacks, while Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.

item.title: Lebanon’s President Joseph Aoun meets Rubio ahead of Trump talks
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Aoun is expected to meet Donald Trump on Tuesday, as Beirut seeks US backing to end Israel-Hezbollah fighting.

item.title: NLC To Push For New Minimum Wage, National Minimum Pension
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Nigeria Labor Congress (NLC) has announced plans to launch a major national campaign to review the national minimum wage and to demand the introduction of a national minimum pension for retirees. NLC president, Comrade Joe Ajaero, disclosed this during the commissioning of the Comrade Godwin Abu

item.title: CSO Seeks Independent Probe Into Death Of NSCDC Officer
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The National Civil Society Council of Nigeria (NCSCN) has called for an independent and impartial investigation into the death of Deputy Superintendent of Corps (DSC) of the Nigeria Security and Civil Defence Corps (NSCDC), Agada Levi Agada, whose passing has generated significant public concern and

item.title: Bauchi LGs Step Up Nutrition Funding As CS-SUNN Intensifies Anti-Stunting Campaign
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Local government councils in Bauchi State are beginning to increase investment in nutrition programmes following sustained advocacy by the Civil Society-Scaling Up Nutrition in Nigeria (CS-SUNN) under the Partnership for Improving Nutrition in Nigeria Systems (PINNS 3.0) project. The advocacy, which
